name: Coquilles Saint-Jacques
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt 4 tablespoons of butter in a saucepan.
2. Add flour, dry mustard, lemon peel, bon appetit, and powdered horseradish to the melted butter. Stir until well blended.
3. Gradually add cream to the mixture. Cook, stirring constantly, until thickened.
4. In a separate pan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter. Add sliced mushrooms and minced onion. Saute until just cooked through.
5. Remove the sauteed mushrooms and onions with a slotted spoon.
6. Return the melted butter to the saucepan. Add the scallops (previously cut into bite-sized pieces) and cook for 3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
7. Add chopped shrimp to the pan and continue cooking until just heated through.
8. Combine the sauce, sauteed mushrooms, onions, scallops, shrimp, and crab meat with dry sherry in a bowl.
9. Spoon the mixture into shells or ramekins.
10. Sprinkle bread crumbs over the top of each serving.
11. Bake at 400°F (200°C) for 10 minutes.
12. Broil briefly until lightly browned.
Serves 8-10 people.
